steps.title: Compute the result
steps.content: Simplify: $$h(-2) = -6 - 7 = -13.$$ That matches choice A — A is correct.

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice A is correct. The value of $h\left(-2\right)$ can be found by substituting $-2$ for $x$ in the equation defining $h$. Substituting $-2$ for $x$ in $h\left(x\right)=3x-7$ yields $h\left(-2\right)=3\left(-2\right)-7$, or $h\left(-2\right)=-13$. Therefore, the value of $h\left(-2\right)$ is $-13$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice B is incorrect. This is the value of $h\left(-1\right)$, not $h\left(-2\right)$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice C is incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.
